tests.intent: The fixed table SQL macro exposes the reviewed bind and result-column contract without overloads.
Production traversal starts at the last node and uses CONNECT BY rather than recursion, dynamic SQL, or procedural loops.
Independent hand calculations cover all axis signs, both non-axis signs, and equality on the partition.
Vertical and horizontal equality follow the explicit sign-sensitive R_PointOnSide-compatible cases.
Non-axis traversal selects side zero only for strictly positive plan-contract cross products.
Traversal begins at maximum node id 680 and its first path component reflects the root decision.
The 0x8000 child flag is recognized and stripped while ordinary node child ids remain nodes.
A leaf sector comes from its first seg and the seg direction selects the facing right or left sidedef.
Pinned player-one thing 157 resolves through the independent path to subsector 115 and sector 140.
Every one of the 292 pinned THINGS coordinates matches its independently decoded subsector, sector, depth, and path tuple.
Points immediately below, exactly on, and immediately above the root partition preserve strict tie semantics.
Large coordinates on both sides traverse deterministically without extent clamping or null output.
Repeated and reordered bind calls produce identical root-to-leaf path signatures and final rows.
Pose coordinates remain macro parameters and caller binds rather than embedded fixture-specific constants.
Production BSP code cannot read evaluator artifacts, embed approved hashes, or branch on test and process state.
